content:
Building HVAC design software: system design for comfortable environment

Heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC) is the core system for building indoor environment control. From load calculation to system design, from pipeline layout to equipment selection, HVAC design software helps engineers scientifically design HVAC systems. This article will comprehensively evaluate mainstream building HVAC design software and help HVAC engineers master the digitalization tool of system design.

Cor of HVAC design software evalue

HVAC design software plays key functions in building design:

  • Load calculation: accurate calculation of building cooling and heating loads
  • System design: HVAC system scheme design
  • Pipeline design: air duct and water pipe network design
  • Equipment selection: HVAC equipment selection and configuration
  • Energy consumption analysis: HVAC system energy consumption prediction

In-depth Review: Mainstream HVAC design software

Carrier HAP: The standard tool for load calculation

Carrier HAP is the HVAC load calculation software launched by Carrier Company.

Core Features:

  • Building cooling and heating load calculation
  • System load analysis
  • Equipment selection suggestions
  • Multiple building type templates
  • Detailed report generation

Load calculation:

Carrier HAP's Coreadvantages are load calculations. The software's load calculation is accurate and is the standard load calculation tool for HVAC engineers.

Equipment selection:

Carrier HAP can recommend Carrier equipment selection based on the load calculation results.

TRACE 700: A comprehensive platform for HVAC design

TRACE 700 is HVAC design and energy consumption analysis software launched by Trane Company.

Core Features:

  • Load calculation and system design
  • Energy consumption analysis
  • Economic analysis
  • Multi-option comparison
  • Detailed report

Comprehensive design:

TRACE The advantages of 700 are comprehensive design. The software integrates load calculation, system design, and energy consumption analysis to provide complete HVAC design solutions.

Economic analysis:

TRACE 700Supported HVAC system economic analysis helps engineers evaluate the cost-effectiveness of the system.

Revit MEP: BIM HVAC design tool

Revit MEP is an Autodesk HVAC design module of the Revit platform.

Core Features:

  • BIM HVAC system modeling
  • 3D design of air ducts and water pipes
  • Equipment family library
  • Collision detection
  • Construction drawing generation

BIMadvantages:

Revit MEP's Corevantages are BIMintegration. HVAC design is integrated into the building BIM model and designed collaboratively with the building and structure.

Three-dimensional design:

Revit MEPSupported three-dimensional design of air ducts and water pipes, and intuitive layout of the piping system.

HVAC design software development trend forecast

AI load forecast

AI will assist load calculation. AI can automatically predict loads based on building parameters and optimize system design. Load calculation efficiency will be improved.

Energy consumption simulation integration

HVAC design software will deeply integrate energy consumption simulation. System design can instantly view energy consumption Effectiveness, optimize energy consumption plan.

BIM collaboration deepens

HVAC design will be deeply integrated into BIM collaboration. HVAC system is coordinated with building and structural models, design conflicts are discovered, and system layout is optimized.

Real-time system simulation

< p>Real-time HVAC system simulation will become possible. Designers can instantly check the effectiveness of system operation and optimize the design plan.

Purchase suggestions

When choosing HVAC design software, it is recommended to consider:

  • Calculation requirements: Professional load calculation requirements are suitable for Carrier HAP
  • BIMapplications: BIM projects are suitable for Revit MEP
  • Design scope: Comprehensive design requires Complete HVAC design platform
  • Equipment brand: Evaluate compatibility with equipment manufacturer software
  • Budget considerations: Evaluate software cost and training investment

HVAC design software is a system design tool for comfortable environments. Choose appropriate HVAC design software and scientifically design HVAC systems.
